Document:
# Autodoc Mk.XIV
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Autodoc"]
The Autodoc Mk.XIV is a programmable surgical machine capable of executing complex medical procedures automatically. It must be linked to an operating table and programmed before use.
[bold][color=#cc3333]WARNING:[/color][/bold] Operation requires proper medical certification and adherence to established protocols. Anesthesia is required for safe operation.
## Setup
Use a multitool to link the Autodoc to a nearby operating table. Once a patient is sleeping on the linked table, the Autodoc is ready to operate.
Place any items a program requires in the Autodoc's storage trays before starting. The machine cannot retrieve items from the floor.
## Programs and Steps
The Autodoc operates through programs — ordered lists of steps executed in sequence until the program completes. If any step fails, the Autodoc aborts the procedure unless safety mode is disabled.
Create a program with a memorable name and add steps using the interface. Available step types:
[bold]PERFORM SURGERY[/bold] — executes a surgery on a chosen body part. Prerequisites such as opening the ribcage are handled automatically. Ensure compatible surgeries are selected — the machine will not prevent logical errors.
[bold]GRAB ITEM[/bold] — retrieves an item from the storage trays by exact name. Use a hand labeler to distinguish multiple copies of the same item.
[bold]GRAB ORGAN[/bold] — retrieves any organ from the storage trays.
[bold]GRAB PART[/bold] — retrieves any body part from the storage trays.
[bold]STORE ITEM[/bold] — places the currently held item back into the storage trays. Required after removing something from a patient, or subsequent steps will drop it on the floor.
[bold]SET LABEL[/bold] — applies a short label to the currently held item. Essential for organ transplants requiring a specific organ.
[bold]WAIT[/bold] — pauses execution for a set duration before continuing.
## Additional Notes
Selecting a step in the program allows you to remove it or insert a new step before it, rather than only appending to the end.
Disabling a program's safety mode causes failed steps to be skipped rather than aborting the entire program. Only disable safety if you understand the full consequences.
If storage trays are full, STORE ITEM steps will fail and the machine will halt. Keep tray space available during long procedures.
Amputating a limb detaches its extremity to the floor. Remove feet and hands manually before amputating arms or legs if they need to be reattached.
